meta.disclaimer: There are no normative 6G specifications yet. As of mid-2026 3GPP 6G is in the study phase; the first 6G specifications are expected in Release 21 (~2029). Every fact below is classified requirement / candidate / foundation and carries a source. Nothing here is a 6G 'spec value'.
meta.baseline_note: Baseline verified against live sources on 2026-06-15. 3gpp.org pages are cited as canonical even where they block automated fetching; figures were cross-checked against ITU-R and reputable secondary sources.

releases.name: Release 20
releases.summary: Dual-track release: continues 5G-Advanced (Rel-20_5GA) while opening the first 6G study track (Rel-20_6G). 6G work in Rel-20 is study-only — scenarios, requirements and technology investigation — not normative specification.

milestones.label: TR 22.870 (Stage-1 6G use cases) approved at TSG SA#111, Fukuoka
TR 38.914 (6G scenarios & requirements) targeted for RAN approval

releases.name: Release 21
releases.summary: Expected to carry the first normative 6G specifications. The Rel-21 timeline is expected to be finalized around mid-2026; an ASN.1/OpenAPI freeze is projected around 2029, with commercial 6G generally expected ~2030. All dates are expectations, not committed 3GPP freeze dates.

milestones.label: Rel-21 timeline expected to be finalized

items.title: Study on 6G Use Cases and Service Requirements (Stage 1)
items.notes: Stage-1 study (~590 pages). Broad and exploratory: immersive communication, hyper-reliable low-latency, more massive IoT, ubiquitous connectivity, AI/ML services, and ISAC. Acts as the Stage-1 anchor for Rel-21 normative work.

items.title: Study on 6G Scenarios and Requirements
items.notes: High-level scenarios and requirements for 6G Radio; guides 3GPP RAN working groups and is an initial input to ITU-R IMT-2030. Covers deployment scenarios (dense urban, industrial, high mobility, NTN), KPIs, spectrum, device types and 5G-to-6G migration.

items.title: Framework and overall objectives of the future development of IMT for 2030 and beyond (IMT-2030)
items.notes: Defines six usage scenarios and 15 capabilities (≈9 with example numeric targets). Numbers in M.2160 are framework example targets, not minimum requirements.

items.title: IMT-2030 Minimum Technical Performance Requirements & Evaluation Methodology
items.status_label: Draft adopted by WP 5D (Feb 2026); SG5 approval scheduled ~Dec 2026
items.notes: The binding minimum performance numbers for 6G evaluation. Draft values remain restricted to ITU-R members until approval, so no minimum-requirement numbers are published here.

items.title: Study on 6G Radio (6GR)
items.notes: Investigates the 6G radio physical layer: waveform, frame structure, numerology for wider bandwidths, channel coding direction, and initial access. Plenary doc ID per secondary catalog; treat as indicative until confirmed on the 3GPP portal.

items.title: 6G baseline waveform: CP-OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM
items.notes: RAN1 has agreed to use CP-OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM as the baseline/benchmark waveform for 6G, while leaving the door open to enhancements. This is a study-phase working assumption, not a frozen specification. Reported via secondary sources.

items.title: Upper mid-band (FR3, 7.125–24.25 GHz) and cmWave (7–15 GHz)
items.notes: The 7–15 GHz cmWave range within FR3 is widely seen as the leading candidate for initial 6G coverage-plus-capacity deployments, complementing FR1 (coverage) and FR2 (bandwidth).

items.title: WRC-27 IMT/6G spectrum studies
items.notes: WRC-23 (Resolution 256) set WRC-27 agenda item 1.2 to study new IMT bands including ranges around 4.4–4.8 GHz, 7.125–8.4 GHz and 14.8–15.35 GHz, with regional candidates such as 6.425–7.125 GHz and 10–10.5 GHz.

items.title: Sub-THz spectrum (100–300 GHz)
items.notes: Sub-THz bands offer very large bandwidth but severe propagation limits; widely framed as a later-phase 6G target (closer to 2035+) rather than initial 2030 deployment.

items.title: AI/ML-native air interface (CSI, beam management, two-sided models)
items.status_label: Study direction carried from Rel-18/19 AI/ML
items.notes: Builds on the Rel-18 study (TR 38.843) and Rel-19 work on AI/ML for the air interface — CSI feedback/compression, beam management, positioning, and two-sided models — toward an AI-native 6G design. The degree of AI-native design in 6G is still under study.

items.title: Integrated Sensing and Communication
items.status_label: Use case agreed (IMT-2030/TR 22.870); RAN design under study
items.notes: ISAC is one of the six IMT-2030 usage scenarios (requirement) and appears in TR 22.870. The RAN realization — waveform reuse, monostatic/bistatic modes, architecture — is a candidate still under study.

items.title: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
items.notes: RIS (smart reflecting surfaces to shape propagation) was studied in 3GPP Rel-19 and is a candidate 6G deployment enabler. No normative 6G RIS specification exists.

items.title: Study on Architecture for the 6G System
items.notes: Investigates the 6G system architecture: a service-based, intent-driven core, an AI-native control plane and NTN/terrestrial convergence. Document number per secondary catalog; treat as indicative until confirmed on the 3GPP portal.

items.title: Non-Terrestrial Networks & ubiquitous connectivity
items.status_label: Usage scenario agreed; 6G integration under study
items.notes: Ubiquitous connectivity is an IMT-2030 usage scenario (requirement) and NTN appears across TR 22.870 and TR 38.914 deployment scenarios. Native terrestrial/NTN convergence in the 6G core and radio is a candidate under study.

items.title: Security, resilience & quantum-era considerations
items.notes: ITU-R M.2160 lists security and resilience as a (qualitative) capability. Post-quantum cryptography migration and AI-era threat models are active research themes; concrete 6G security specification is future Rel-21+ work.

domains.name: 6G Vision & Requirements
domains.summary: IMT-2030 usage scenarios and capabilities; the 3GPP Stage-1 (TR 22.870) and RAN (TR 38.914) requirement studies.

domains.name: 6G Radio (6GR) Physical Layer
domains.summary: Waveform and numerology candidates, a unified 5G/6G frame structure, channel coding direction, and initial access.

domains.name: 6G Spectrum
domains.summary: Upper mid-band (FR3) and cmWave, sub-THz, and the WRC-27 spectrum agenda.

domains.name: AI/ML-Native Air Interface
domains.summary: Machine learning built into the radio: CSI feedback, beam management, positioning and two-sided models.

domains.name: Integrated Sensing & Communication
domains.summary: Using the network as a radar: sensing modes, waveform reuse and use cases.

domains.name: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
domains.summary: Programmable surfaces that reshape the propagation environment.

domains.name: Non-Terrestrial Networks
domains.summary: Satellite and aerial integration toward ubiquitous connectivity.

domains.name: 6G Core & Architecture
domains.summary: A service-based, intent-driven, AI-native 6G core and terrestrial/NTN convergence.

domains.name: Security & Quantum-Era
domains.summary: Resilience, the AI-era threat model, and post-quantum cryptography migration.

changelog.summary: Initial 6G tracker baseline established from verified primary (3GPP, ITU-R) and labelled secondary sources.
TR 22.870 (Stage-1 6G use cases) recorded as approved at TSG SA#111 (Fukuoka, March 2026).
TR 38.914 (6G scenarios & requirements) recorded at ~60% complete (March 2026), approval targeted ~June 2026.
